samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelined processing. Implement sequential logic for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation. Ensure proper synchronization and output assignment.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling multiplication.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating multiplication status.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output with a width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD.</state>
        <state id="CALCULATE">State CALCULATE.</state>
        <state id="OUTPUT">State OUTPUT.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[1:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                mul_en_out_reg <= 2'b0;
                mul_a_reg <= 8'b0;
                mul_b_reg <= 8'b0;
                temp[0] <= 16'b0;
                temp[1] <= 16'b0;
                temp[2] <= 16'b0;
                temp[3] <= 16'b0;
                temp[4] <= 16'b0;
                temp[5] <= 16'b0;
                temp[6] <= 16'b0;
                temp[7] <= 16'b0;
                sum[0] <= 16'b0;
                sum[1] <= 16'b0;
                sum[2] <= 16'b0;
                sum[3] <= 16'b0;
                sum[4] <= 16'b0;
                sum[5] <= 16'b0;
                sum[6] <= 16'b0;
                sum[7] <= 16'b0;
                mul_out_reg <= 16'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update the output enable register with the input enable signal
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[0], mul_en_in};
                
                // Update the multiplicand register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[0]) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                end
                
                // Update the multiplier register with the input value
                if (mul_en_out_reg[0]) begin
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
                
                // Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits
                temp[0] <= mul_b_reg[0] ? {8'b0, mul_a_reg} : 16'b0;
                temp[1] <= mul_b_reg[1] ? {7'b0, mul_a_reg, 1'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[2] <= mul_b_reg[2] ? {6'b0, mul_a_reg, 2'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[3] <= mul_b_reg[3] ? {5'b0, mul_a_reg, 3'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[4] <= mul_b_reg[4] ? {4'b0, mul_a_reg, 4'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[5] <= mul_b_reg[5] ? {3'b0, mul_a_reg, 5'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[6] <= mul_b_reg[6] ? {2'b0, mul_a_reg, 6'b0} : 16'b0;
                temp[7] <= mul_b_reg[7] ? {1'b0, mul_a_reg, 7'b0} : 16'b0;
                
                // Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= sum[0] + temp[1];
                sum[2] <= sum[1] + temp[2];
                sum[3] <= sum[2] + temp[3];
                sum[4] <= sum[3] + temp[4];
                sum[5] <= sum[4] + temp[5];
                sum[6] <= sum[5] + temp[6];
                sum[7] <= sum[6] + temp[7];
                
                // Accumulate the final product
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3] + sum[4] + sum[5] + sum[6] + sum[7];
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the output product based on the output enable signal
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out_reg[1] ? mul_out_reg : 16'b0;
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Sequential logic blocks for input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation.</implementation>
endmodule
